rationale: The evidence shows a coordinated, multi-step ransomware/destructive-attack preparation sequence executed within ~850ms from C:\Temp\ under corp\Administrator at High integrity: (A0) vssadmin.exe deletes all Volume Shadow Copies to eliminate recovery points; (EV-002, EV-003) bcdedit disables boot status policy and Windows Recovery Environment to prevent OS self-repair; (EV-004, EV-005) wevtutil clears the System and Security event logs to destroy forensic evidence; and (EV-001) the Security audit log is confirmed cleared (Event 1102). All commands originate from cmd.exe parents in C:\Temp\, a non-standard working directory consistent with a dropped payload orchestrating these steps. The combination of shadow copy deletion, recovery disablement, and log wiping is a canonical pre-encryption or post-compromise cleanup pattern with no plausible benign explanation.
